title: Yang-Mills mass gap attack round 2 — 2D lattice Wilson loop + confining potential + Ising-dual mass gap

honesty_charter.output_type: exact numerical verification of 2D-solvable YM analogs
honesty_charter.prior_status: Round 1 (BT-543_round_1.json) delivered REFINE-A/B/C conjecture refinement of beta_0 = sigma(6)-sopfr(6). Sibling round-2 file bt543_yang_mills.hexa performs n=6 numerology scan. This attack is orthogonal: strictly numerical lattice gauge theory in d=2 where exact results exist.

angle_shift_from_r1_and_sibling.r1_style: structural/analytical refinement of beta_0 sigma-sopfr conjecture
angle_shift_from_r1_and_sibling.sibling_style: arithmetic coincidence scanner (SU(N) x n_f grid, Lie ranks, perfect 28)
angle_shift_from_r1_and_sibling.this_r2_style: exact 2D lattice gauge numerical computation — area law, confining potential, mass gap positivity, beta_c self-duality
angle_shift_from_r1_and_sibling.rationale: R1 + sibling cover arithmetic angle. This attack probes the ONLY dimension where YM mass gap has rigorous mathematical results (Balaban 1988-89, Chatterjee 2020) and validates hexa math stack on the resulting exact formulas.

A_wilson_area_law.label: VERIFIED
A_wilson_area_law.probe: 2D Z_2 pure gauge strong-coupling Wilson loop <W(R,T)> = tanh(beta)^(R*T)
A_wilson_area_law.theory_ref: Wilson K. (1974). Confinement of Quarks. Phys. Rev. D 10:2445-2459.
A_wilson_area_law.measurement: Compute W(R,T) for R,T in {1,2,3} at beta in {0.1, 0.3, 0.5, 0.75, 1.0, 1.5, 2.0}; extract sigma(beta) = -log(tanh(beta)) and verify area law fit
A_wilson_area_law.verdict: area law holds exactly in strong-coupling 2D Z_2 — no surprise, tautological identity check, confirms hexa ln_/tanh_/exp_ stack

B_static_potential.label: DERIVED
B_static_potential.probe: V(r) = -log<W(r,T)>/T as T -> infinity, should equal sigma * r (linear confinement)
B_static_potential.measurement: Compute V(r) for r in 1..5 at beta=0.5; verify linearity
B_static_potential.verdict: V(r) exactly linear in r at fixed beta — confinement in 2D Z_2 verified. Not a Clay-problem input (2D is solvable).

C_mass_gap_ising_dual.label: DERIVED
C_mass_gap_ising_dual.probe: Kramers-Wannier duality 2D Z_2 gauge <-> 2D Ising; disordered phase mass gap m(beta) = -log(tanh(beta)) > 0 for beta < beta_c
C_mass_gap_ising_dual.theory_ref: Kramers H., Wannier G. (1941). Statistics of the 2D Ising model. Phys. Rev. 60:252-262. Wegner F. (1971). Duality in generalized Ising models. J. Math. Phys. 12:2259.
C_mass_gap_ising_dual.measurement: Compute m_gap at beta in {0.1, 0.2, 0.3, 0.4}, all below beta_c = 0.4407

result.verdict: gap strictly positive in 2D disordered phase

C_mass_gap_ising_dual.verdict: 2D Z_2 mass gap POSITIVITY exactly verified via duality. Clay 4D SU(3) mass gap positivity remains OPEN.

D_beta_critical_self_duality.label: VERIFIED
D_beta_critical_self_duality.probe: 2D Z_2 gauge self-duality fixed point: sinh(2*beta_c) = 1 -> beta_c = (1/2)*log(1+sqrt(2))
D_beta_critical_self_duality.theory_ref: Wegner F. (1971). Duality in generalized Ising models. J. Math. Phys. 12:2259-2272.
D_beta_critical_self_duality.measurement: Compute beta_c via formula, verify sinh(2*beta_c)=1
D_beta_critical_self_duality.verdict: self-duality fixed point exact to machine precision — hexa sqrt_/ln_/sinh_ sufficient for 2D exact results

E_dimensional_reduction_honesty.label: HONEST_MISS
literature_status: Balaban T. (1988-89): 2D and 3D continuum YM rigorously constructed (CMP 116, 122).
Magnen-Rivasseau-Seneor (1993): 4D partial construction conditional on UV cutoff.
Chatterjee S. (2016, 2020): 2D stochastic YM rigorous existence (Commun. Math. Phys.).
4D pure YM mass gap: UNRESOLVED (Clay Millennium Problem).
gap_to_4d: (i) SU(N) Haar integration with full gauge-group structure (beyond Z_2 finite group)
(ii) Monte Carlo heat-bath updater for numerical sampling (not attempted)
(iii) Finite-size continuum extrapolation via asymptotic freedom running
(iv) Osterwalder-Schrader reflection positivity for Hamiltonian spectrum reconstruction
(v) Mass gap lower bound that survives the continuum limit
E_dimensional_reduction_honesty.verdict: HONEST_MISS — 2D toy is numerically clean but carries no derivational force toward 4D Clay. This was already documented in R1 CE-1.

edge_cases_discovered: ln_() Taylor instability on tiny arguments (W -> 0 at small beta). Workaround: use analytic identity ln(a^n) = n*ln(a) instead of computing W then log W.
exp_() Taylor 60-term expansion fine for |x| < ~10; larger x needs range reduction.
missing_for_4d_scaling: Haar measure integration for compact Lie groups (SU(2), SU(3))
Pseudo-RNG for Monte Carlo heat-bath (no stdlib rand_uniform in current hexa)
Multi-dimensional array of gauge link variables (not attempted but doable via flat arrays)
hexa_capability_verdict.overall_verdict: hexa SUFFICIENT for 2D exactly-solvable lattice YM; INSUFFICIENT STDLIB for 4D Monte Carlo lattice gauge (needs RNG + Haar sampling). Not a fundamental language limitation.

honesty_statement.text: Round 2 verifies 2D Z_2 lattice gauge exact results (Wilson area law, confining V(r), Ising-dual mass gap positivity, self-duality fixed point) to machine precision using pure-hexa math primitives. These are EXACT CONSEQUENCES of 1941-1974 results already in literature; no new physics or math is claimed. The 4D Clay Yang-Mills mass gap remains UNRESOLVED, as explicitly noted in probes E and the hexa_capability_verdict. BT-543 resolution remains 0/1 MISS. Key deliverable: hexa math stack is suitable for lattice-gauge toy computation but lacks stdlib RNG/Haar sampling for realistic 4D Monte Carlo. Capability gap documented.

round_3_options.direction: 3D Z_2 lattice gauge: dual to 3D Ising model; phase structure known but gap expression less clean. Test hexa on 3D L^3 = 4^3 = 64 site lattice explicit Monte Carlo.
round_3_options.blockers: needs stdlib pseudo-RNG (LCG acceptable) and gauge-link flat array representation.

round_3_options.direction: SU(2) 4D plaquette action Monte Carlo on 2^4 lattice, extract glueball correlator, attempt exponential decay fit. Highly speculative at this size (finite-size dominant).
round_3_options.blockers: Haar sampling on SU(2) via quaternion + RNG; complex.

round_3_options.direction: Large-N (t Hooft) 2D YM exact partition function (Migdal 1975, Witten 1991) on torus, verify Z = sum over reps formula.
round_3_options.blockers: needs character expansion; closed-form doable in hexa.

round_3_options.direction: Polyakov loop winding number on 2D cylinder, link to deconfinement transition. Exactly solvable.

next_round_candidates.recommended: R3-C (large-N 2D YM exact) or R3-D (Polyakov winding) — both exact, cheap, orthogonal to sibling.
